title: Write the Email Asking for the Raise
subtitle: A writing activity for putting the ask on paper before you say it out loud
excerpt: A short written activity: you draft the email, message, or script asking for the raise or rate increase you want, including the specific number. You don't send it yet. You read it back and notice what it feels like to see the ask in writing.

practice.whenToUse: Use when a raise conversation is coming and the words have not been found yet — or when the conversation keeps being postponed.

content.text: A writing activity for getting the ask onto paper before it needs to be said. Writing the raise request in full, including the actual number, makes the ask less abstract and gives the fear something concrete to work with.
New to activities as practice? Read

Open a blank document. Write the email, message, or script you would send or say to ask for the raise or rate increase — including the specific number or percentage you want. Write it plainly, without excessive justification.
Don't send it. Read it back once. Notice what it feels like to see the ask in writing. If the number shrank as you wrote it, write the number you originally intended alongside and sit with both.
The draft is preparation, not the conversation itself. Use the journal prompts or tapping in this library to work with what the reading-back reveals.
